commands.review_summary:
The PR exports `loadCronJobForShow`, adds a 50-page/non-advancing `cron.list` guard for `cron show`, and adds regression tests for stuck, capped, later-page, and empty pagination.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main loops forever over `cron.list` pages when `hasMore` is true and `nextOffset` does not advance. I did not execute a live current-main reproduction because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: The PR has no blocking code-review finding, but the external contributor needs real behavior proof or maintainer override before merge.

target.title: fix(cron-cli): bound loadCronJobForShow pagination (#83856)
target.body:
Fixes #83856.

## Summary

`loadCronJobForShow` was paginating with `for (;;)` — no max-pages cap, no check that `nextOffset` actually advances. If the gateway ever returns `hasMore=true` with `nextOffset` stuck at the same value, `openclaw cron show <id-or-name>` would loop forever, reissuing identical `cron.list` RPCs.

The sibling `loadCronJobForEditSchedulePatch` in `register.cron-edit.ts` already has this guard; this PR mirrors that pattern in `register.cron-simple.ts`:

- cap at `CRON_SHOW_LOOKUP_MAX_PAGES = 50` iterations
- assert `nextOffset > offset` between pages, throw if not
- throw if the page cap is exceeded

Also exports `loadCronJobForShow` so the regression test can target it directly (the issue specifically calls out that this feature group has no tests).

AI-assisted (Claude Code).

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/cron-cli` — 25/25 pass, including the four new regression tests
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/cli/cron-cli/register.cron-simple.ts src/cli/cron-cli/register.cron-simple.test.ts` — 0 warnings, 0 errors
- `oxfmt --check` on both touched files — clean
- `pnpm tsgo:core` — clean

### Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** `openclaw cron show <name>` would hang indefinitely if the gateway returned a `cron.list` page with `hasMore=true` and a non-advancing `nextOffset` (e.g. stuck at 0), reissuing the same RPC forever.
- **Real environment tested:** local macOS checkout, rebased on upstream `main` (d60ab485).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/cron-cli/register.cron-simple.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/cron-cli` (broader sweep — full cron-cli test files)
  - `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/cli/cron-cli/register.cron-simple.ts src/cli/cron-cli/register.cron-simple.test.ts`
  - `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/cli/cron-cli/register.cron-simple.ts src/cli/cron-cli/register.cron-simple.test.ts`
- **Evidence after fix:** the new regression test mocks `callGatewayFromCli` to return `{ jobs: [], hasMore: true, nextOffset: 0 }` and asserts `loadCronJobForShow` rejects with `pagination did not advance` after exactly one RPC instead of looping. A second test forces 50 advancing pages and asserts the page-cap throw fires. A third test verifies a multi-page successful lookup still works, and a fourth covers the empty-result termination path.
- **Observed result after fix:** 25/25 cron-cli tests pass; pre-fix, the non-advancing case would hang (and timed out under vitest).
- **What was not tested:** I did not reproduce the hang against a real misbehaving gateway end-to-end — the unit test covers the same code path with a mocked transport, which is what the issue itself recommends.
